patch.value: Make MCP status enum + shell completion runtime-resolved from schema (not hardcoded)

Config-driven schema gaps (audit). The MCP tool 'status' parameter is pinned to the 6 default statuses (tool-parameter-tables.ts ~47), so a project with custom statuses in schema/statuses.json cannot pass them via MCP even though the CLI accepts them — breaks never-block parity (the MCP 'type' param already correctly uses an unconstrained runtime-validated string; status should follow). Also shell completion uses the hardcoded BUILTIN_ITEM_TYPE_VALUES (completion.ts ~25/37) so custom types never appear in bash/zsh/fish type completion (completion is static — at least document that custom types exist). Make lifecycle enums runtime-resolved where the transport allows.
Custom statuses accepted via MCP; completion documents/handles custom types
